headline: Week’s Best: Investing in Energy as the War Winds Down
summary: The Iran war sent oil and gas prices surging and sharpened investors’ focus on energy investments.  Now that the conflict appears likely to end, the question is how to play the sector: Some investors are leaning into traditional oil-patch names, while others favor pipelines and natural gas.  For a recent Barron’s Advisor Big Q, we asked a panel of investment professionals how they’re putting money to work in energy now.

headline: Schwab and Robinhood Stocks Get a Boost on SpaceX IPO Day
summary: Shares of several retail brokers that offered shares of SpaceX to customers at the IPO price rose on Friday, although not as dramatically as SpaceX's more than 20% gain.  Charles Schwab shares were up 3% in late afternoon trading Friday, although the company also had some non-SpaceX good news of its own that probably helped boost the stock: solid growth in new assets and brokerage accounts.  Robinhood's stock gained more than 1.5%, despite the fact that the firm reported some technical issues earlier Friday due to the "record-breaking traffic."

headline: Schwab Asset Management Announces Closure and Liquidation of the Schwab Ariel Opportunities ETF (SAEF)
summary: WESTLAKE, Texas, June 12, 2026--At a meeting held on June 10, 2026, upon the recommendation of Charles Schwab Investment Management, Inc. ("CSIM"), the investment adviser to the fund, the Board of Trustees of the Schwab Strategic Trust approved the closure and liquidation of the Schwab Ariel Opportunities ETF (SAEF). The fund will cease trading on the NYSE Arca, Inc. (NYSE) as of the close of regular trading on July 17, 2026 (the "Closing Date"), at which time the fund’s shares will be delisted.

headline: A successful SpaceX IPO could signal the reopening of capital markets, says Citizens' Devin Ryan
summary: Devin Ryan, Citizens head of financial technology research and equity research analyst, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss the upcoming SpaceX IPO, state of the IPO market, impact of SpaceX IPO on retail investing, and more.

headline: Why retail investors want in on the SpaceX IPO despite valuation risks
summary: SpaceX is expected to go public at one of the richest IPO valuations in market history, with retail investors set to receive unusual access through platforms like Robinhood and Charles Schwab. CNBC spoke with five retail traders and investors about why they requested SpaceX IPO shares despite concerns over valuation, volatility, Elon Musk, Starlink, AI infrastructure, launch services and whether public-market buyers could become exit liquidity for early investors.

headline: SpaceX IPO: Here's what retails investors need to know
summary: Retail brokerages are lining up to offer SpaceX shares to retail investors who are salivating to buy day one of trade. However, any investor who is thinking of flipping the stock and making a quick buck may want to think twice. Fidelity and Charles Schwab have implemented anti-flipping policies ahead of the historic IPO. CNBC's Seema Mody analyzed other high-profile IPOs, and explains what is unique about SpaceX, to find out what to expect.
